data.title: Agios falls after mixed data from late-stage trial for sickle cell therapy
data.description:
Shares of Agios Pharmaceuticals (AGIO) plunged ~45% in the premarket on Wednesday after the biotech announced a mixed outcome from a late-stage trial for its lead asset mitapivat in sickle cell disease, a rare, inherited blood disorder.

Cambridge, Massachusetts-based Agios (AGIO) noted that based on topline data, its RISE UP Phase 3 trial met its primary endpoint of hemoglobin response, with mitapivat indicating a statistically significant improvement compared to placebo.

However, there was no statistically significant reduction in the annualized rate of sickle cell pain crises, another primary endpoint, and the 207-patient trial also failed in a key secondary endpoint, the company said.

The placebo-controlled trial was designed to evaluate mitapivat, marketed as Pyrukynd, for anemia in adults with pyruvate kinase ((PK)) deficiency in patients aged 16 years or older with sickle cell disease.

While RISE UP demonstrated statistically significant improvements in the first two key secondary endpoints, 20.3% of patients on mitapivat experienced Serious treatment-emergent adverse events compared to 29.0% in the placebo arm.

Three deaths were reported in the on-drug arm compared to two in the control arm, while 4.3% on mitapivat discontinued the therapy due to TEAEs compared to 2.9% on placebo.

However, Agios (AGIO) said it would meet with the U.S. FDA in Q1 2026 and submit a marketing application for the oral pyruvate kinase activator in SCD.

The company added that it plans to cut expenses to maintain its solid financial profile and support the potential U.S. commercial rollout of Pyrukynd in thalassemia, and will issue an update in this regard early next year.

data.title: Dycom to acquire Power Solutions for $1.95B to boost data center infrastructure services
data.description:
• Dycom Industries (DY) said on Wednesday it has agreed to acquire Power Solutions, a major Mid-Atlantic electrical contractor serving data centers, in a deal valuing the company at $1.95 billion.
  • The acquisition will expand its presence in fast-growing digital and data-center infrastructure markets and add more than 2,800 skilled employees.
  • Power Solutions, based in Bowie, Maryland, is expected to generate about $1 billion in revenue in 2025 and has maintained mid-to-high-teens adjusted EBITDA margins, supported by a backlog exceeding $1 billion.

  • The purchase price includes about $293 million in Dycom stock, with the remainder paid in cash.

  • Dycom plans to finance the deal with cash on hand, a $1 billion term loan A facility and a $700 million bridge loan. The company expects pro forma net leverage to be below 3.0x at closing and to fall to around 2.0x within 12 to 18 months.

  • Power Solutions will continue to operate under its existing brand and management team.

  • Dycom said the deal will be immediately accretive to adjusted EBITDA margin and adjusted diluted EPS and improve free cash flow.

  • DY +8.67% premarket to $321.9
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Iron ore rises to two-week high on firm demand and reduced supply from China
data.description:
Iron ore futures rose to their highest in more than two weeks on Wednesday, helped by strong demand and declining domestic supply in top consumer China.

According to Reuters, the January contract for iron ore (SCO:COM) on China's Dalian Commodity Exchange closed daytime trading +0.7% at 791.5 yuan/metric ton ($111.32), after touching its highest level since November 3 at 797 yuan/ton, and benchmark December iron ore traded on the Singapore Exchange was +0.1% to $104.55/ton after hitting its highest since November 4 at $104.95/ton.

Chinese steel mills have used a low-inventory strategy for raw materials during the past three years as margins were squeezed by falling steel prices alongside the protracted property downturn, which analysts say means that mills will need to return to the spot market to restock cargoes more frequently, thus improving spot liquidity, which to some extent acted as a buffer during a price slump.

Lower domestic supply also is supporting iron ore prices, as official data released this week showed China's run-of mine - raw ore to be processed to concentrate and pellets - fell in October by 2.9% Y/Y to 84 million tons.

Potentially relevant stocks include BHP (BHP), Rio Tinto (RIO), Vale (VALE), Antofagasta (OTC:ANFGF), Glencore (CLNF) (OTCPK:GLNCY) and Anglo American (OTCQX:AAUKF) (OTCQX:NGLOY).

data.title: TJX Companies gains after sailing past comparable sales expectations
data.description:
TJX Companies (TJX) pushed higher in early trading on Wednesday after the retailer reported comparable sales growth of 5% in Q3 to smash the consensus expectation of +3.6%. Total revenue was up 7% year-over-year to $15.1 billion for the quarter that ended on November 1.

The Marmaxx business saw comparable sales rise 6% during Q3, while the HomeGoods business saw a 5% comparable sales increase during the period. Comparable sales were up 8% for TJX Canada and were 3% higher for TJX International (Europe and Australia).

During Q3, gross profit margin was 32.6% vs. 31.6% a year ago, driven by a higher merchandise margin and expense leverage on sales. Selling, general and administrative costs as a percent of sales were 20.1 vs. 19.5% a year ago. EPS was reported at $1.28 vs. $1.22 consensus and $1.14 a year ago.

TJX (TJX) generated $1.5 billion of operating cash flow and ended the quarter with $4.6 billion of cash.

TJX's (TJX) inventory position at the end of the quarter was $9.4 billion, vs. $8.4 billion a year ago. Consolidated inventories on a per-store basis, including distribution centers, but excluding inventory in transit and the company’s e-commerce sites, were up 8% on both a reported and constant currency basis versus last year. The inventory position was noted to reflect the "terrific" buying opportunities TJX (TJX) saw in the third quarter.

CEO Ernie Herrman: "The fourth quarter is off to a strong start, the availability of merchandise continues to be outstanding, and we are excited about the deals we are seeing in the marketplace. With our compelling values and ever-changing, fresh assortments of good, better, and best brands, we are convinced that our stores and e-commerce sites are strongly positioned as gifting destinations for value-conscious shoppers this holiday season."

Looking ahead, guidance for the key holiday quarter was set by TJX (TJX) for consolidated comparable sales to be up 2% to 3% and EPS to land in a range of $1.33 to $1.36 (midpoint $1.345) vs. $1.37 consensus.

Shares of TJX Companies (TJX) were up 2.6% in premarket trading to $149.30 vs. the 52-week range of $112.10 to $148.11.

data.title: Larry Summers resigns from OpenAI's board over release of Epstein emails
data.description:
• Former Treasury Secretary, Harvard President and Director of the National Economic Council Larry Summers has resigned from OpenAI's (OPENAI) board of directors over the release of emails between him and convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.
  • “I am grateful for the opportunity to have served, excited about the potential of the company, and look forward to following their progress,” Summers told CNBC.
  • OpenAI's board of directors issued its own statement, saying it “appreciate[s] his many contributions and the perspective he brought to the Board.”
  • Emails between Summers and Epstein were released publicly as part of more than 20,000 documents that the House Oversight and Government Reform Committee obtained via a subpoena.
  • OpenAI has not yet responded to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

(This is breaking news...)
